response.justification: Bogdan has a long and impressive career in full-stack and distributed systems engineering, but he falls critically short on the role's hard requirements. The minimum qualification of 8 years of C++ experience is not met — his C++ exposure is limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2007-2008 and a bachelor thesis project. His primary languages throughout his career have been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. While he has some distributed systems experience (Sociomantic, GISCollective on Kubernetes) and demonstrates a culture of engineering craft and mentorship, the gap in C++ depth and cloud platform leadership makes him a poor fit for this specific role.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering experience across distributed systems, embedded, and full-stack domains, including high-throughput microservices at Sociomantic
Demonstrated engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD advocacy, CI/CD pipelines, code katas, workshops, and staff-level mentoring
Staff-level contributor with leadership exposure: cross-team tooling, AI-agent infrastructure, and open-source platform development at re:cinq
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months (NI, 2007-2008), far below the 8-year minimum requirement — a disqualifying gap for this role
No cloud platform architecture ownership; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is operational-level, not platform-building or infrastructure strategy
No formal technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineering teams, which is a preferred qualification and implicit expectation of the role
